lint.command: ROOT=/home/realnigga/Desktop/Mission-control && cd "$ROOT" && bash ci/lint-knowledgebase.sh && bash ci/check-architecture.sh && bash ci/check-enterprise-policy.sh && bash ci/check-orchestrator-layer.sh && python3 scripts/check_flaky_tags.py --tests-root erp-domain/src/test/java --gate gate-fast && bash scripts/guard_openapi_contract_drift.sh

blockingIssues.description: The ownership hard-cut is still incomplete because `AccountingCoreEngineCore` keeps full business-logic implementations for the same touched behaviors the fix was supposed to extract, including `createStandardJournal`, `createJournalEntry`, `postPayrollRun`, `recordPayrollPayment`, `recordSupplierPayment`, `autoSettleSupplier`, `settleDealerInvoices`, and `settleSupplierInvoices`, leaving duplicate live owners instead of one canonical owner per behavior.

`AccountingIdempotencyService` still exposes public `recordSupplierPayment`, `settleDealerInvoices`, `autoSettleDealer`, `settleSupplierInvoices`, and `autoSettleSupplier` methods that delegate via `super.*` back into `AccountingCoreEngineCore`, so the indirect monolith delegation chain was not fully hard-cut.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: When repo-wide Spotless drift is a known pre-existing condition, document scoped formatter verification with `mvn spotless:check -DspotlessFiles=...` as the expected touched-files-only fallback instead of leaving workers to record it as an ad hoc deviation.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The `accounting-split-receipt-replay-contention-proof` review cited a justified deviation to rerun Spotless only for the touched test file, and the earlier `accounting-direct-journal-payroll-settlement-extraction` handoff reported the same scoped-Spotless workaround because the mission guidance already treats full-repo Spotless drift as pre-existing.

rejectedObservations.observation: The accounting module AGENTS guidance should be updated immediately to describe `JournalEntryService` and `PayrollAccountingService` as the new canonical owners instead of `AccountingCoreEngineCore`.
rejectedObservations.reason: ambiguous: this rerun still fails because `AccountingCoreEngineCore` and `AccountingIdempotencyService` retain duplicate live owners and shadow delegation paths, so updating the module guidance now would misstate the current runtime ownership instead of clarifying it.

rejectedObservations.observation: Shared worker guidance should explicitly bless detached-worktree clean-status verification when the root checkout contains unrelated dirty files that cannot be touched.
rejectedObservations.reason: duplicate: the same mission-worker-base guidance update was already recommended in round 4, and this review did not add a new distinct remediation beyond reaffirming that existing recommendation.
